beats.text: You learned to solve problems that need two steps. You know to find the combined total first, then compare. Let's see what you know.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. School Supplies Sold. Scale of 5, axis 0-50. Pencils 40, Erasers 25, Notebooks 35, Markers 20.

beats.text: How many more pencils were sold than markers?
How many more pencils were sold than markers?

validator.description: Student selected 20, correct (40 - 20 = 20)

beats.text: Right. 20 more pencils sold. This one asked about only two categories and was just one step—no combined, so straight to comparing.

validator.description: Student selected 60 (added pencils and markers instead of finding difference)

beats.text: How many more means compare, not add. Look at how far apart the bars are. What's the difference between 40 and 20?

validator.description: Student selected 40 (identified pencils value but did not subtract)

beats.text: That's the pencils total. How many more asks for the difference. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. How much bigger is 40 than 20?

validator.description: Student selected 15 (compared wrong categories or miscalculated)

beats.text: The question asks about pencils and markers. Find those two bars. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. Subtract to find how many more.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Comparison overlay shows pencils bar at 40 and markers bar at 20, with difference of 20 highlighted.

beats.text: Let me show you. How many more means compare. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. I subtract to find the difference: 40 minus 20 equals 20. The answer is 20. That's what compare means—find the difference between two values.

params.title: Books Read This Month
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Books Read This Month. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. Fiction 50, Nonfiction 30, Comics 40, Poetry 20.

beats.text: How many more fiction books were read than nonfiction and poetry combined?
What should we find first?

validator.description: Student selected add nonfiction and poetry, correct first step

beats.text: Right. Combine nonfiction and poetry first.

validator.description: Student selected compare fiction to nonfiction, skipping the combined step

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

beats.text: The question asks about nonfiction and poetry combined. When you see combined or together, add those first. What's the total of nonfiction and poetry?

validator.description: Student selected add all four categories, including categories not needed

beats.text: We only need nonfiction and poetry. The question doesn't ask about comics or fiction yet. Add the two categories the question names first.

validator.description: Student selected subtract poetry from nonfiction, incorrect operation

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

beats.text: Combined means add, not subtract. When the question says nonfiction and poetry together, we find the total by adding. What do those two add up to?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

beats.text: Let me show you how to think about this. The question says nonfiction and poetry combined. That word combined tells us to add those two first. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. Together they make 50. We need to find that combined total first before we can compare anything. The answer is add nonfiction and poetry.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Books Read This Month. Scale of 10. Fiction 50, Nonfiction 30, Comics 40, Poetry 20.

params.description: Nonfiction bar and Poetry bar highlight.

beats.text: What's the combined total of nonfiction and poetry?
How many nonfiction and poetry books combined?

validator.description: Student answered 50, correct, 30 plus 20

beats.text: That's right. 50 combined. That's nonfiction 30 plus poetry 20.

validator.description: Student selected 10, the difference between nonfiction and poetry

params.description: Both Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

beats.text: You found the difference between nonfiction and poetry. But we need the total of both together. Combined means add. What's 30 plus 20?

validator.description: Student selected 40, the value for comics

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight, not Comics.

beats.text: That's the comics value. We need nonfiction and poetry. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. Add those two.

validator.description: Student selected 60, possibly adding fiction instead of the correct categories

beats.text: Look at the highlighted bars. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. What do those two add up to?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates: Nonfiction bar (30) + Poetry bar (20) = 50

beats.text: Here's how this works. The question asks for nonfiction and poetry combined. I look at nonfiction: that bar reaches 30. I look at poetry: that bar reaches 20. Combined means I add them. 30 plus 20 equals 50. The combined total is 50.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Books Read This Month. Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlighted. Scale of 10.

beats.text: Nonfiction and poetry combined is 50. How many fiction books were read? Find how many more.
How many more fiction books than nonfiction and poetry combined?

validator.description: Student answered 0, correct, fiction equals combined total, no difference

beats.text: Exactly. Fiction is 50, combined is 50. Same amount. No difference.

validator.description: Student selected 100, adding fiction to the combined total instead of comparing

beats.text: You added fiction to the combined total. The question asks how many more, so we compare. Fiction is 50, combined is 50. What's the difference between those?

validator.description: Student selected 50, giving one of the values instead of the difference

beats.text: That's how many fiction books there are. But we need to compare. Fiction is 50, the combined total is 50. They're equal. What's the difference when two numbers are equal?

validator.description: Student selected 10, possibly subtracting poetry from nonfiction instead of comparing fiction to combined

beats.text: Fiction is 50. The combined total of nonfiction and poetry is 50. When both amounts are the same, there is no difference. How many more is 50 than 50?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows Fiction bar (50) compared to combined total (50), demonstrating they are equal

beats.text: Let me walk you through this. We found that nonfiction and poetry combined equals 50. Now I look at fiction: that bar reaches 50. The question asks how many more fiction than the combined total. I compare: 50 minus 50 equals 0. When two amounts are equal, the difference is zero. Fiction and the combined total are the same, so the answer is 0.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Scale of 5, axis 0-60. Context: Votes for Field Trip. Five categories: Aquarium 35, Museum 25, Zoo 45, Park 30, Farm 15.

beats.text: How many fewer votes did the museum and farm combined get than the zoo?
What should we find first?

validator.description: Student selected to add museum and farm first, correct strategy

beats.text: Right. Combine museum and farm first.

validator.description: Student selected wrong strategy on first attempt

beats.text: Look at the question. It says museum and farm combined. What does combined tell us to do?

validator.description: Student selected wrong strategy on second attempt

beats.text: The question asks about museum and farm together. When you see combined or together, add those categories first. Which choice shows adding museum and farm?

validator.description: Student selected wrong strategy three or more times. System models the correct strategy.

beats.text: Let me show you. The question says museum and farm combined. Combined means add. So our first step is to add museum and farm together. That's what we need to find first.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Votes for Field Trip data. Five categories displayed.

params.description: Museum bar (25) and Farm bar (15) highlight.

beats.text: What's the combined total of votes for the museum and farm?
How many votes did museum and farm get combined?

validator.description: Student answered 40, correct (25 + 15 = 40)

beats.text: That's it. 40 combined. That's 25 plus 15.

validator.description: Student selected 10, likely subtracted 25 - 15 instead of adding

beats.text: You subtracted. But the question asks for combined. Combined means add the two values. What is 25 plus 15?

validator.description: Student selected 30, possibly misread one bar value

params.description: Museum and Farm bars pulse to show values.

beats.text: Check the bar heights. Museum is 25, farm is 15. Count by fives or add them. What is the total?

validator.description: Student selected 45, the zoo value instead of the combined total

beats.text: That's the zoo's votes. We need the combined total for museum and farm. Add those two bars. Museum is 25, farm is 15. What do they add up to?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

beats.text: Here's how we find it. Museum is 25 votes. Farm is 15 votes. We add them: 25 plus 15 equals 40. The combined total for museum and farm is 40 votes.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Museum and Farm bars highlighted. Votes for Field Trip data.

params.description: Zoo bar (45) highlights. Combined value 40 displayed.

beats.text: Museum and farm combined is 40. Now find how many fewer votes they got than the zoo.
How many fewer votes did the museum and farm combined get than the zoo?

validator.description: Student answered 5, correct (45 - 40 = 5)

beats.text: You got it. 5 fewer. Museum and farm combined got 5 fewer votes than the zoo. You used two steps: first added, then compared.

validator.description: Student selected 85, likely added 45 + 40 instead of subtracting

beats.text: You added. But the question asks how many fewer. Fewer means compare, and compare means subtract. The zoo has 45, combined is 40. What is the difference?

validator.description: Student selected 45, the zoo value without comparing

beats.text: That's just the zoo. We need to compare. Zoo is 45, combined is 40. Find the difference between those two numbers.

validator.description: Student selected 40, the combined value without comparing

params.description: Zoo bar highlights. Combined value 40 displayed.

beats.text: That's the combined total we found. Now we compare it to the zoo. The zoo has 45 votes. How many more is 45 than 40?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Shows: 45 compared to 40, difference of 5 highlighted

beats.text: Let me walk through it. Museum and farm combined is 40 votes. The zoo has 45 votes. We compare: 45 minus 40 equals 5. Museum and farm combined got 5 fewer votes than the zoo. The answer is 5.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Books Read This Week data. Scale of 2. Maya=14, Ben=9, Carla=11, David=6.

beats.text: Different graph, different scale. Use what you know.
How many books did Maya and Ben read together?

validator.description: Student selected 23, correct (14 + 9 = 23)

beats.text: Right. 23 books together. You added Maya's 14 and Ben's 9.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Read the scale and add Maya's books and Ben's books.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly on second attempt

beats.text: Here's what to do: count by twos on Maya's bar to 14, then count Ben's bar to 9. Add those numbers. What is 14 plus 9?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

beats.text: Let me show you. The scale is 2, so I count by twos. Maya's bar reaches 14. Ben's bar reaches 9. When we see together, we add. 14 plus 9 equals 23. Maya and Ben read 23 books together.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Books Read This Week data. Scale of 2. Maya=14, Ben=9, Carla=11, David=6.

beats.text: Now compare that total to Carla.
How many MORE books did Maya and Ben read together than Carla?

validator.description: Student selected 12, correct (23 - 11 = 12)

beats.text: That's it. 12 more. You found the combined total first, then compared.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly on first attempt

beats.text: Let's look closely. You have 23 total. Carla has 11. Compare them.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly on second attempt

beats.text: Here's the key: you found that Maya and Ben together is 23. How many more means we subtract. Count Carla's bar: 11. What is 23 minus 11?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

beats.text: Let me walk you through it. Maya and Ben together is 23 books. Carla read 11 books. How many more asks for the difference. We subtract: 23 minus 11 equals 12. The answer is 12 books more.
You're ready to practice.
